components.role: Source follower transistor — provides high input impedance and unity voltage gain
Gate bias resistor — sets input impedance to ~1MΩ
Source resistor — sets bias current and output impedance
Input coupling capacitor — blocks DC, sets low-frequency rolloff
Output coupling capacitor — blocks DC from source node

dsp_model.description: Unity gain with first-order highpass from input coupling capacitor

behavioral_description: A transparent, high-impedance input buffer that preserves the guitar signal without loading the pickups. The JFET source follower provides approximately unity voltage gain with very high input impedance (~1MΩ), preventing treble loss from cable capacitance and pickup loading. The tone is slightly warm and organic compared to op-amp buffers, with a subtle JFET character that many players find pleasing. The low-frequency rolloff is set by the coupling capacitor and keeps subsonic rumble out of the signal chain.
